326	//Creates the timing graph nodes for the associated primary I/O
327	void TimingGraphBuilder::add_io_to_timing_graph(const AtomBlockId blk) {
328	    NodeType node_type;
329	    AtomPinId pin;
330	    if (netlist_.block_type(blk) == AtomBlockType::INPAD) {
331	        node_type = NodeType::SOURCE;
332	
333	        if (netlist_.block_pins(blk).size() == 1) {
334	            pin = *netlist_.block_pins(blk).begin();
335	        } else {
336	            //Un-swept disconnected input
337	            VTR_ASSERT(netlist_.block_pins(blk).size() == 0);
338	            return;
339	        }
340	
341	    } else {
342	        VTR_ASSERT(netlist_.block_type(blk) == AtomBlockType::OUTPAD);
343	        node_type = NodeType::SINK;
344	
345	        if (netlist_.block_pins(blk).size() == 1) {
346	            pin = *netlist_.block_pins(blk).begin();
347	        } else {
348	            //Un-swept disconnected output
349	            VTR_ASSERT(netlist_.block_pins(blk).size() == 0);
350	            return;
351	        }
352	    }
353	
354	    NodeId tnode = tg_->add_node(node_type);
355	
356	    netlist_lookup_.set_atom_pin_tnode(pin, tnode, BlockTnode::EXTERNAL);
357	}
358	
359	//Creates the timing graph nodes and internal edges for a netlist block
360	void TimingGraphBuilder::add_block_to_timing_graph(const AtomBlockId blk) {
361	    /*
362	     * How the code builds the primitive timing sub-graph
363	     * -------------------------------------------------
364	     *
365	     * The code below builds the timing sub-graph corresponding to the
366	     * current netlist primitive/block. This is accomplished by walking through
367	     * the primitive's input, clock and output pins and creating the corresponding
368	     * tnodes (note that if internal sequential paths exist within the primitive
369	     * this also creates the appropriate internal tnodes).
370	     *
371	     * Once all nodes have been created the edges are added between them according
372	     * to what was specified in the architecture file models.
373	     *
374	     * Note that to minimize the size of the timing graph we only create tnodes and
375	     * edges where they actually exist within the netlist. This means we do not create
376	     * tnodes or tedges to/from pins which are disconnected in the netlist (even if
377	     * they exist in the architecture).
378	     *
379	     *
380	     * Clock Generators
381	     * ----------------
382	     * An additional wrinkle in the above process is the presence of clock generators,
383	     * such as PLLs, which may define new clocks at their output (in contrast with a
384	     * primary input which is always a SOURCE type tnode).
385	     *
386	     * If a primitive output pin is marked as a clock then we create a SOURCE
387	     * type node instead of the regular OPIN.
388	     *
389	     * Additionally, in some instances designs use 'data' signals as clocks (usually
390	     * bad practise, but it happens). We detect such cases and also create a
391	     * SOURCE (and leave any combinational inputs to that node disconnected).
392	     */
393	
394	    auto clock_generator_tnodes = create_block_timing_nodes(blk);
395	    create_block_internal_data_timing_edges(blk, clock_generator_tnodes);
396	    create_block_internal_clock_timing_edges(blk, clock_generator_tnodes);
397	}
398	
399	//Constructs the timing graph nodes for the specified block
400	//
401	//Returns the set of created tnodese which are clock generators
402	std::set<tatum::NodeId> TimingGraphBuilder::create_block_timing_nodes(const AtomBlockId blk) {
403	    std::set<std::string> output_ports_used_as_combinational_sinks;
404	
405	    //Create the tnodes corresponding to input pins
406	    for (AtomPinId input_pin : netlist_.block_input_pins(blk)) {
407	        AtomPortId input_port = netlist_.pin_port(input_pin);
408	
409	        //Inspect the port model to determine pin type
410	        const t_model_ports* model_port = netlist_.port_model(input_port);
411	
412	        NodeId tnode;
413	        VTR_ASSERT(!model_port->is_clock);
414	        if (model_port->clock.empty()) {
415	            //No clock => combinational input
416	            tnode = tg_->add_node(NodeType::IPIN);
417	
418	            //A combinational pin is really both internal and external, mark it internal here
419	            //and external in the default case below
420	            netlist_lookup_.set_atom_pin_tnode(input_pin, tnode, BlockTnode::INTERNAL);
421	        } else {
422	            //This is a sequential data input (i.e. a sequential data capture point/timing path end-point)
423	            tnode = tg_->add_node(NodeType::SINK);
424	
425	            if (!model_port->combinational_sink_ports.empty()) {
426	                //There is an internal combinational connection starting at this sequential input
427	                //pin. This is a new timing path and hence we must create a new SOURCE node.
428	
429	                //Create the internal source
430	                NodeId internal_tnode = tg_->add_node(NodeType::SOURCE);
431	                netlist_lookup_.set_atom_pin_tnode(input_pin, internal_tnode, BlockTnode::INTERNAL);
432	            }
433	        }
434	
435	        //Record any output port which will be used as a combinational sink
436	        // This is used to determine (when creating output pin tnodes) whether we also need to
437	        // create an internal SINK tnode.
438	        output_ports_used_as_combinational_sinks.insert(model_port->combinational_sink_ports.begin(),
439	                                                        model_port->combinational_sink_ports.end());
440	
441	        //Save the pin to external tnode mapping
442	        netlist_lookup_.set_atom_pin_tnode(input_pin, tnode, BlockTnode::EXTERNAL);
443	    }
444	
445	    //Create the clock pins
446	    for (AtomPinId clock_pin : netlist_.block_clock_pins(blk)) {
447	        AtomPortId clock_port = netlist_.pin_port(clock_pin);
448	
449	        //Inspect the port model to determine pin type
450	        const t_model_ports* model_port = netlist_.port_model(clock_port);
451	
452	        VTR_ASSERT(model_port->is_clock);
453	        VTR_ASSERT(model_port->clock.empty());
454	
455	        NodeId tnode = tg_->add_node(NodeType::CPIN);
456	
457	        netlist_lookup_.set_atom_pin_tnode(clock_pin, tnode, BlockTnode::EXTERNAL);
458	    }
459	
460	    //Create the output pins
461	    std::set<tatum::NodeId> clock_generator_tnodes;
462	    for (AtomPinId output_pin : netlist_.block_output_pins(blk)) {
463	        AtomPortId output_port = netlist_.pin_port(output_pin);
464	
465	        //Inspect the port model to determine pin type
466	        const t_model_ports* model_port = netlist_.port_model(output_port);
467	
468	        NodeId tnode;
469	        if (is_netlist_clock_source(output_pin)) {
470	            //A generated clock source
471	            tnode = tg_->add_node(NodeType::SOURCE);
472	
473	            clock_generator_tnodes.insert(tnode);
474	
475	            if (!model_port->is_clock) {
476	                //An implicit clock source, possibly clock derived from data
477	
478	                AtomNetId clock_net = netlist_.pin_net(output_pin);
479	                VTR_LOG_WARN("Inferred implicit clock source %s for netlist clock %s (possibly data used as clock)\n",
480	                             netlist_.pin_name(output_pin).c_str(), netlist_.net_name(clock_net).c_str());
481	
482	                //This type of situation often requires cutting paths between the implicit clock source and
483	                //it's inputs which can cause dangling combinational nodes. Do not error if this occurs.
484	                tg_->set_allow_dangling_combinational_nodes(true);
485	            }
486	        } else {
487	            VTR_ASSERT_MSG(!model_port->is_clock, "Primitive data output (i.e. non-clock source output pin) should not be marked as a clock generator");
488	
489	            if (model_port->clock.empty()) {
490	                //No clock => combinational output
491	                tnode = tg_->add_node(NodeType::OPIN);
492	
493	                //A combinational pin is really both internal and external, mark it internal here
494	                //and external in the default case below
495	                netlist_lookup_.set_atom_pin_tnode(output_pin, tnode, BlockTnode::INTERNAL);
496	
497	            } else {
498	                VTR_ASSERT(!model_port->clock.empty());
499	                //Has an associated clock => sequential output
500	                tnode = tg_->add_node(NodeType::SOURCE);
501	
502	                if (output_ports_used_as_combinational_sinks.count(model_port->name)) {
503	                    //There is a combinational path within the primitive terminating at this sequential output
504	
505	                    //Create the internal sink node
506	                    NodeId internal_tnode = tg_->add_node(NodeType::SINK);
507	                    netlist_lookup_.set_atom_pin_tnode(output_pin, internal_tnode, BlockTnode::INTERNAL);
508	                }
509	            }
510	        }
511	
512	        //Record as external tnode
513	        netlist_lookup_.set_atom_pin_tnode(output_pin, tnode, BlockTnode::EXTERNAL);
514	    }
515	
516	    return clock_generator_tnodes;
517	}
518	
519	void TimingGraphBuilder::create_block_internal_clock_timing_edges(const AtomBlockId blk, const std::set<tatum::NodeId>& clock_generator_tnodes) {
520	    //Connect the clock pins to the sources and sinks
521	    for (AtomPinId pin : netlist_.block_pins(blk)) {
522	        for (auto blk_tnode_type : {BlockTnode::EXTERNAL, BlockTnode::INTERNAL}) {
523	            NodeId tnode = netlist_lookup_.atom_pin_tnode(pin, blk_tnode_type);
524	            if (!tnode) continue;
525	
526	            if (clock_generator_tnodes.count(tnode)) continue; //Clock sources don't have incoming clock pin connections
527	
528	            auto node_type = tg_->node_type(tnode);
529	
530	            if (node_type != NodeType::SOURCE && node_type != NodeType::SINK) continue;
531	
532	            VTR_ASSERT_SAFE(node_type == NodeType::SOURCE || node_type == NodeType::SINK);
533	
534	            //Look-up the clock name on the port model
535	            AtomPortId port = netlist_.pin_port(pin);
536	            const t_model_ports* model_port = netlist_.port_model(port);
537	
538	            VTR_ASSERT_MSG(!model_port->clock.empty(), "Sequential pins must have a clock");
539	
540	            //Find the clock pin in the netlist
541	            AtomPortId clk_port = netlist_.find_port(blk, model_port->clock);
542	            VTR_ASSERT(clk_port);
543	            VTR_ASSERT_MSG(netlist_.port_width(clk_port) == 1, "Primitive clock ports can only contain one pin");
544	
545	            AtomPinId clk_pin = netlist_.port_pin(clk_port, 0);
546	            VTR_ASSERT(clk_pin);
547	
548	            //Convert the pin to its tnode
549	            NodeId clk_tnode = netlist_lookup_.atom_pin_tnode(clk_pin);
550	            VTR_ASSERT(clk_tnode);
551	
552	            //Determine the type of edge to create
553	            //This corresponds to how the clock (clk_tnode) relates
554	            //to the data (tnode). So a SOURCE data tnode should be
555	            //a PRIMTIVE_CLOCK_LAUNCH (clock launches data), while
556	            //a SINK data tnode should be a PRIMITIVE_CLOCK_CAPTURE
557	            //(clock captures data).
558	            tatum::EdgeType type;
559	            if (node_type == NodeType::SOURCE) {
560	                type = tatum::EdgeType::PRIMITIVE_CLOCK_LAUNCH;
561	            } else {
562	                VTR_ASSERT(node_type == NodeType::SINK);
563	                type = tatum::EdgeType::PRIMITIVE_CLOCK_CAPTURE;
564	            }
565	
566	            //Add the edge from the clock to the source/sink
567	            tg_->add_edge(type, clk_tnode, tnode);
568	        }
569	    }
570	
571	    //Connect the combinational edges from clock input pins
572	    //
573	    //These are typically used to represent clock buffers
574	    for (AtomPinId src_clock_pin : netlist_.block_clock_pins(blk)) {
575	        NodeId src_tnode = netlist_lookup_.atom_pin_tnode(src_clock_pin, BlockTnode::EXTERNAL);
576	
577	        if (!src_tnode) continue;
578	
579	        //Look-up the combinationally connected sink ports name on the port model
580	        AtomPortId src_port = netlist_.pin_port(src_clock_pin);
581	        const t_model_ports* model_port = netlist_.port_model(src_port);
582	
583	        for (const std::string& sink_port_name : model_port->combinational_sink_ports) {
584	            AtomPortId sink_port = netlist_.find_port(blk, sink_port_name);
585	            if (!sink_port) continue; //Port may not be connected
586	
587	            //We now need to create edges between the source pin, and all the pins in the
588	            //output port
589	            for (AtomPinId sink_pin : netlist_.port_pins(sink_port)) {
590	                //Get the tnode of the sink
591	                NodeId sink_tnode = netlist_lookup_.atom_pin_tnode(sink_pin, BlockTnode::EXTERNAL);
592	
593	                tg_->add_edge(tatum::EdgeType::PRIMITIVE_COMBINATIONAL, src_tnode, sink_tnode);
594	                VTR_LOG("Adding edge from '%s' (tnode: %zu) -> '%s' (tnode: %zu) to allow clocks to propagate\n", netlist_.pin_name(src_clock_pin).c_str(), size_t(src_tnode), netlist_.pin_name(sink_pin).c_str(), size_t(sink_tnode));
595	            }
596	        }
597	    }
598	}
599	
600	void TimingGraphBuilder::create_block_internal_data_timing_edges(const AtomBlockId blk, const std::set<tatum::NodeId>& clock_generator_tnodes) {
601	    //Connect the combinational edges from data input pins
602	    //
603	    //These edges may represent an intermediate (combinational) sub-path of a
604	    //timing path (i.e. between IPINs and OPINs), the start of a timing path (i.e. SOURCE
605	    //to OPIN), the end of a timing path (i.e. IPIN to SINK), or an internal timing path
606	    //(i.e. SOURCE to SINK).
607	    //
608	    //Note that the creation of these edges is driven by the 'combinational_sink_ports' specified
609	    //in the architecture primitive model
610	    for (AtomPinId src_pin : netlist_.block_input_pins(blk)) {
611	        //Note that we have already created all the relevant nodes, and appropriately labelled them as
612	        //internal/external. As a result, we only need to consider the 'internal' tnodes when creating
613	        //the edges within the current block.
614	        NodeId src_tnode = netlist_lookup_.atom_pin_tnode(src_pin, BlockTnode::INTERNAL);
615	
616	        if (!src_tnode) continue;
617	
618	        NodeType src_type = tg_->node_type(src_tnode);
619	
620	        //Look-up the combinationally connected sink ports name on the port model
621	        AtomPortId src_port = netlist_.pin_port(src_pin);
622	        const t_model_ports* model_port = netlist_.port_model(src_port);
623	        VTR_ASSERT_SAFE(model_port != nullptr);
624	
625	        for (const std::string& sink_port_name : model_port->combinational_sink_ports) {
626	            AtomPortId sink_port = netlist_.find_port(blk, sink_port_name);
627	            if (!sink_port) continue; //Port may not be connected
628	
629	            //We now need to create edges between the source pin, and all the pins in the
630	            //output port
631	            for (AtomPinId sink_pin : netlist_.port_pins(sink_port)) {
632	                //Get the tnode of the sink
633	                NodeId sink_tnode = netlist_lookup_.atom_pin_tnode(sink_pin, BlockTnode::INTERNAL);
634	
635	                if (!sink_tnode) {
636	                    //No tnode found, either a combinational clock generator or an error
637	
638	                    //Try again looking for an external tnode
639	                    sink_tnode = netlist_lookup_.atom_pin_tnode(sink_pin, BlockTnode::EXTERNAL);
640	
641	                    //Is the sink a clock generator?
642	                    if (sink_tnode && clock_generator_tnodes.count(sink_tnode)) {
643	                        //Do not create the edge
644	                        VTR_LOG_WARN("Timing edge from %s to %s will not be created since %s has been identified as a clock generator\n",
645	                                     netlist_.pin_name(src_pin).c_str(), netlist_.pin_name(sink_pin).c_str(), netlist_.pin_name(sink_pin).c_str());
646	                    } else {
647	                        //Unknown
648	                        VPR_FATAL_ERROR(VPR_ERROR_TIMING, "Unable to find matching sink tnode for timing edge from %s to %s",
649	                                        netlist_.pin_name(src_pin).c_str(), netlist_.pin_name(src_pin).c_str());
650	                    }
651	
652	                } else {
653	                    //Valid tnode create the edge
654	                    auto sink_type = tg_->node_type(sink_tnode);
655	
656	                    VTR_ASSERT_MSG((src_type == NodeType::IPIN && sink_type == NodeType::OPIN)
657	                                       || (src_type == NodeType::SOURCE && sink_type == NodeType::SINK)
658	                                       || (src_type == NodeType::SOURCE && sink_type == NodeType::OPIN)
659	                                       || (src_type == NodeType::IPIN && sink_type == NodeType::SINK),
660	                                   "Internal primitive combinational edges must be between {IPIN, SOURCE} and {OPIN, SINK}");
661	
662	                    //Add the edge between the pins
663	                    tg_->add_edge(tatum::EdgeType::PRIMITIVE_COMBINATIONAL, src_tnode, sink_tnode);
664	                }
665	            }
666	        }
667	    }
668	}
669	
670	void TimingGraphBuilder::add_net_to_timing_graph(const AtomNetId net) {
671	    //Create edges from the driver to sink tnodes
672	
673	    AtomPinId driver_pin = netlist_.net_driver(net);
674	
675	    if (!driver_pin) {
676	        //Undriven nets have no timing dependencies, and hence no edges
677	        VTR_LOG_WARN("Net %s has no driver and will be ignored for timing purposes\n", netlist_.net_name(net).c_str());
678	        return;
679	    }
680	
681	    NodeId driver_tnode = netlist_lookup_.atom_pin_tnode(driver_pin);
682	    VTR_ASSERT(driver_tnode);
683	
684	    for (AtomPinId sink_pin : netlist_.net_sinks(net)) {
685	        NodeId sink_tnode = netlist_lookup_.atom_pin_tnode(sink_pin);
686	        VTR_ASSERT(sink_tnode);
687	
688	        tg_->add_edge(tatum::EdgeType::INTERCONNECT, driver_tnode, sink_tnode);
689	    }
690	}
691	
692	void TimingGraphBuilder::fix_comb_loops() {
693	    //Loops correspond to Strongly Connected Components (SCCs) in the graph
694	    auto sccs = tatum::identify_combinational_loops(*tg_);
695	
696	    //For non-simple loops (i.e. SCCs with multiple loops) we may need to break
697	    //multiple edges, so repeatedly break edges until there are no SCCs left
698	    while (!sccs.empty()) {
699	        VTR_LOG_WARN("Detected %zu strongly connected component(s) forming combinational loop(s) in timing graph\n", sccs.size());
700	        for (const auto& scc : sccs) {
701	            EdgeId edge_to_break = find_scc_edge_to_break(scc);
702	            VTR_ASSERT(edge_to_break);
703	
704	            tg_->disable_edge(edge_to_break);
705	        }
706	
707	        sccs = tatum::identify_combinational_loops(*tg_);
708	    }
709	}
710	
711	tatum::EdgeId TimingGraphBuilder::find_scc_edge_to_break(std::vector<tatum::NodeId> scc) {
712	    //Find an edge which is part of the SCC and remove it from the graph,
713	    //in the hope of breaking the SCC
714	    std::set<tatum::NodeId> scc_set(scc.begin(), scc.end());
715	    for (tatum::NodeId src_node : scc) {
716	        AtomPinId src_pin = netlist_lookup_.tnode_atom_pin(src_node);
717	        for (tatum::EdgeId edge : tg_->node_out_edges(src_node)) {
718	            if (tg_->edge_disabled(edge)) continue;
719	
720	            tatum::NodeId sink_node = tg_->edge_sink_node(edge);
721	            AtomPinId sink_pin = netlist_lookup_.tnode_atom_pin(sink_node);
722	
723	            if (scc_set.count(sink_node)) {
724	                VTR_LOG_WARN("Arbitrarily disabling timing graph edge %zu (%s -> %s) to break combinational loop\n",
725	                             edge, netlist_.pin_name(src_pin).c_str(), netlist_.pin_name(sink_pin).c_str());
726	                return edge;
727	            }
728	        }
729	    }
730	    return tatum::EdgeId::INVALID();
731	}
732	
733	void TimingGraphBuilder::remap_ids(const tatum::GraphIdMaps& id_mapping) {
734	    //Update the pin-tnode mapping
735	    for (BlockTnode type : {BlockTnode::EXTERNAL, BlockTnode::INTERNAL}) {
736	        for (auto kv : netlist_lookup_.atom_pin_tnodes(type)) {
737	            AtomPinId pin = kv.first;
738	            tatum::NodeId old_tnode = kv.second;
739	
740	            if (!old_tnode) continue;
741	
742	            tatum::NodeId new_tnode = id_mapping.node_id_map[old_tnode];
743	
744	            netlist_lookup_.set_atom_pin_tnode(pin, new_tnode, type);
745	        }
746	    }
747	}
748	
749	bool TimingGraphBuilder::is_netlist_clock_source(const AtomPinId pin) const {
750	    return netlist_clock_drivers_.count(pin);
751	}
752	
753	bool TimingGraphBuilder::validate_netlist_timing_graph_consistency() const {
754	    for (AtomPinId pin : netlist_.pins()) {
755	        tatum::NodeId ext_tnode = netlist_lookup_.atom_pin_tnode(pin, BlockTnode::EXTERNAL);
756	        if (!ext_tnode) VPR_ERROR(VPR_ERROR_TIMING, "Found no external tnode for atom pin '%zu'", size_t(pin));
757	
758	        tatum::NodeId int_tnode = netlist_lookup_.atom_pin_tnode(pin, BlockTnode::INTERNAL);
759	
760	        /*
761	         * Sanity check look-up consistency
762	         */
763	        AtomPinId ext_tnode_pin = netlist_lookup_.tnode_atom_pin(ext_tnode);
764	        if (ext_tnode_pin != pin) {
765	            VPR_ERROR(VPR_ERROR_TIMING, "Inconsistent external tnode -> atom pin lookup: atom pin %zu -> tnode %zu, but tnode %zu -> atom pin %zu",
766	                      size_t(pin), size_t(ext_tnode), size_t(ext_tnode), size_t(ext_tnode_pin));
767	        }
768	        if (int_tnode) {
769	            AtomPinId int_tnode_pin = netlist_lookup_.tnode_atom_pin(int_tnode);
770	            if (int_tnode_pin != pin) {
771	                VPR_ERROR(VPR_ERROR_TIMING, "Inconsistent internal tnode -> atom pin lookup: atom pin %zu -> tnode %zu, but tnode %zu -> atom pin %zu",
772	                          size_t(pin), size_t(int_tnode), size_t(int_tnode), size_t(int_tnode_pin));
773	            }
774	        }
775	
776	        /*
777	         * Sanity check internal/external tnode types
778	         */
779	        tatum::NodeType ext_tnode_type = tg_->node_type(ext_tnode);
780	        if (ext_tnode_type == tatum::NodeType::IPIN || ext_tnode_type == tatum::NodeType::OPIN) {
781	            if (!int_tnode) VPR_ERROR(VPR_ERROR_TIMING, "Missing expected internal tnode for combinational atom pin %zu", size_t(pin));
782	            if (int_tnode != ext_tnode) VPR_ERROR(VPR_ERROR_TIMING, "Mismatch  external/internal tnodes (%zu vs %zu) for combinational atom pin %zu",
783	                                                  size_t(ext_tnode), size_t(int_tnode), size_t(pin));
784	        } else if (ext_tnode_type == tatum::NodeType::CPIN) {
785	            if (int_tnode) VPR_ERROR(VPR_ERROR_TIMING, "Unexpected internal tnode (%zu) for clock pin: atom pin %zu ,external tnode %zu",
786	                                     size_t(int_tnode), size_t(pin), size_t(ext_tnode));
787	        } else if (ext_tnode_type == tatum::NodeType::SOURCE) {
788	            if (int_tnode && tg_->node_type(int_tnode) != tatum::NodeType::SINK) {
789	                VPR_ERROR(VPR_ERROR_TIMING, "Found internal tnode (%zu) associated with atom pin %zu, but it is not a SINK (external tnode %zu was a SOURCE)",
790	                          size_t(int_tnode), size_t(pin), size_t(ext_tnode));
791	            }
792	
793	        } else if (ext_tnode_type == tatum::NodeType::SINK) {
794	            if (int_tnode && tg_->node_type(int_tnode) != tatum::NodeType::SOURCE) {
795	                VPR_ERROR(VPR_ERROR_TIMING, "Found internal tnode (%zu) associated with atom pin %zu, but it is not a SOURCE (external tnode %zu was a SINK)",
796	                          size_t(int_tnode), size_t(pin), size_t(ext_tnode));
797	            }
798	        } else {
799	            VPR_ERROR(VPR_ERROR_TIMING, "Unexpected timing node type for atom pin %zu", size_t(pin));
800	        }
801	    }
802	
803	    return true; //success
804	}
